oracle_sources.name: won_info_field
oracle_sources.rationale: The 'won' field is the benchmark's authoritative binary success signal; it is recorded per-step in every trajectory and directly indicates whether the PDDL goal was fully satisfied, making it the canonical oracle for episode-level success detection.

oracle_sources.name: admissible_commands_validity_heuristic
oracle_sources.rationale: The per-step admissible_commands list is the exact set of PDDL-valid actions at each state; comparing consecutive steps allows detection of invalid actions (action at step N not in admissible_commands at step N-1) and inference of held-object state (presence of 'move <obj>' commands implies agent holds that object).

oracle_sources.name: traj_data_task_annotation
oracle_sources.rationale: The traj_data.json pddl_params field provides the ground-truth target object (object_target), target receptacle (parent_target), and lamp/appliance identifier (toggle_target) for each game, enabling exact string-matching against action sequences rather than fragile heuristic pattern extraction.

task_types.rationale_for_tests: The pick-and-place task has three necessary sub-goals: (1) the agent locates and picks up the target object, (2) the agent navigates to the target receptacle, and (3) the agent places the object there. Each sub-goal is independently detectable from the action sequence and location field. An avoided-error test measuring the invalid-action rate captures aimless or stuck behavior without relying on observation text. Together these four tests form a fine-grained progress ladder that awards partial credit for reaching earlier milestones even on failed episodes.

unit_tests.name: target_object_picked_up
unit_tests.description: Agent executed a 'take <object_target> from ...' action at least once during the episode, indicating it successfully picked up the task-relevant object.

detector.predicate: obj = pddl_params['object_target'].lower(); any(step['action'].lower().startswith('take ') and obj in step['action'].lower() for step in steps)

unit_tests.rationale: Picking up the target is the irreducible first step; a failing agent that never reaches this milestone is qualitatively different from one that grasps the right object but fails to place it.

unit_tests.name: target_receptacle_visited
unit_tests.description: Agent's 'location' field at some step starts with or contains the parent_target name (lowercased), indicating it navigated to the correct receptacle.

detector.predicate: recep = pddl_params['parent_target'].lower(); any(recep in step['location'].lower() for step in steps)

unit_tests.rationale: Reaching the destination receptacle is a distinct navigation milestone; an agent that holds the object but wanders never contributes to task completion.

unit_tests.name: target_placed_at_receptacle
unit_tests.description: Agent executed a 'move <object_target> to <parent_target>' action, indicating it placed the target object at the correct destination.

detector.predicate: obj = pddl_params['object_target'].lower(); recep = pddl_params['parent_target'].lower(); any((step['action'].lower().startswith('move ') or step['action'].lower().startswith('put ')) and obj in step['action'].lower() and recep in step['action'].lower() for step in steps)

unit_tests.rationale: Placing the target object at the correct receptacle is the terminal milestone directly preceding task success; detecting it awards the largest weight for this task type.

unit_tests.name: low_invalid_action_rate
unit_tests.description: Fewer than 30% of actions from step 1 onward were not in the admissible_commands list of the preceding step (i.e., produced 'Nothing happens.' feedback).

detector.predicate: invalid = sum(1 for i in range(1, len(steps)) if steps[i]['action'] not in steps[i-1]['admissible_commands']); total = max(1, len(steps) - 1); (invalid / total) < 0.30

unit_tests.rationale: A high rate of invalid actions signals an agent that is guessing or producing hallucinated commands; this test rewards coherent action selection without revealing the solution path.

task_types.rationale_for_tests: The look-at-object-in-light task requires: (1) finding and picking up the target object, (2) locating the desklamp, (3) activating the lamp while holding the target. The third sub-goal is the hardest to detect without PDDL facts, but it can be approximated by checking whether a 'use <toggle_target>' action occurred while admissible_commands from the previous step contained a 'move <object_target>' command (which implies the agent was holding the object). An avoided-error test on invalid actions complements these milestone tests.

unit_tests.name: target_object_picked_up
unit_tests.description: Agent executed a 'take <object_target> from ...' action at least once, indicating it grasped the task-relevant object.

detector.predicate: obj = pddl_params['object_target'].lower(); any(step['action'].lower().startswith('take ') and obj in step['action'].lower() for step in steps)

unit_tests.rationale: Picking up the target object is the first necessary step; without it the lamp-examination goal cannot be satisfied.

unit_tests.name: lamp_activated
unit_tests.description: Agent executed a 'use <toggle_target>' action at any point in the episode.

detector.predicate: lamp = pddl_params['toggle_target'].lower(); any(step['action'].lower().startswith('use ') and lamp in step['action'].lower() for step in steps)

unit_tests.rationale: Interacting with the lamp is a necessary precondition for satisfying the look-under-light goal; detecting it independently from holding the object reveals whether the agent understood the lamp sub-goal.

unit_tests.name: lamp_used_while_holding_target
unit_tests.description: There exists a step N where action is 'use <toggle_target>' AND admissible_commands at step N-1 include a 'move <object_target>' command (indicating the agent held the target when the lamp was activated).

detector.predicate: lamp = pddl_params['toggle_target'].lower(); obj = pddl_params['object_target'].lower(); any(i > 0 and steps[i]['action'].lower().startswith('use ') and lamp in steps[i]['action'].lower() and any(cmd.lower().startswith('move ' + obj) for cmd in steps[i-1]['admissible_commands']) for i in range(len(steps)))

unit_tests.rationale: This is the key compound milestone: the agent must simultaneously hold the target AND activate the lamp; detecting both conditions together is a reliable proxy for PDDL goal satisfaction without needing the facts oracle. Leakage risk is low because the offline scorer never feeds this result back as text to the agent.

unit_tests.name: low_invalid_action_rate
unit_tests.description: Fewer than 30% of actions from step 1 onward were not in the admissible_commands list of the preceding step.

detector.predicate: invalid = sum(1 for i in range(1, len(steps)) if steps[i]['action'] not in steps[i-1]['admissible_commands']); total = max(1, len(steps) - 1); (invalid / total) < 0.30

unit_tests.rationale: A high rate of invalid actions indicates the agent is emitting hallucianted commands rather than planning; this error class is especially common when the agent cannot find the lamp.

task_types.rationale_for_tests: This task has a strict pipeline: pickup → clean at sinkbasin → place at receptacle. The ordering constraint is the distinguishing feature vs. pick_and_place_simple. Five unit tests capture the three milestone stages, the pipeline ordering as an efficiency signal, and invalid-action rate. Detecting the 'clean X with sinkbasin' action (the PDDL clean verb) from the action string is reliable since the action grammar always uses this form.

unit_tests.name: target_object_picked_up
unit_tests.description: Agent executed a 'take <object_target> from ...' action at least once.

detector.predicate: obj = pddl_params['object_target'].lower(); any(step['action'].lower().startswith('take ') and obj in step['action'].lower() for step in steps)

unit_tests.rationale: Picking up the target is the prerequisite for all subsequent sub-goals; missing this milestone means the agent never started the task pipeline.

unit_tests.name: target_object_cleaned
unit_tests.description: Agent executed a 'clean <object_target> with ...' action at least once.

detector.predicate: obj = pddl_params['object_target'].lower(); any(step['action'].lower().startswith('clean ') and obj in step['action'].lower() for step in steps)

unit_tests.rationale: The clean step is the task-distinguishing transformation; detecting it confirms the agent understood the task requires cleaning before placement.

unit_tests.name: cleaned_object_placed_at_receptacle
unit_tests.description: Agent executed a 'move <object_target> to <parent_target>' action at least once.

detector.predicate: obj = pddl_params['object_target'].lower(); recep = pddl_params['parent_target'].lower(); any((step['action'].lower().startswith('move ') or step['action'].lower().startswith('put ')) and obj in step['action'].lower() and recep in step['action'].lower() for step in steps)

unit_tests.rationale: Placing the target object at the correct receptacle is the final milestone; it receives the highest weight as it is closest to task completion.

unit_tests.name: correct_pipeline_order
unit_tests.description: The first take-target step occurs before the first clean-target step, and the first clean-target step occurs before the first place-target-at-recep step. All three must be detected for this test to pass.

detector.predicate: obj = pddl_params['object_target'].lower(); recep = pddl_params['parent_target'].lower(); pickup_i = next((i for i,s in enumerate(steps) if s['action'].lower().startswith('take ') and obj in s['action'].lower()), -1); clean_i = next((i for i,s in enumerate(steps) if s['action'].lower().startswith('clean ') and obj in s['action'].lower()), -1); place_i = next((i for i,s in enumerate(steps) if (s['action'].lower().startswith('move ') or s['action'].lower().startswith('put ')) and obj in s['action'].lower() and recep in s['action'].lower()), -1); pickup_i != -1 and clean_i != -1 and place_i != -1 and pickup_i < clean_i < place_i

unit_tests.rationale: The pipeline ordering bonus reveals whether the agent executed the sub-goals in the correct sequence; an agent that places before cleaning cannot succeed. Leakage risk is low because ordering information is not more than what the task description already implies.

unit_tests.name: low_invalid_action_rate
unit_tests.description: Fewer than 30% of actions from step 1 onward were not in the admissible_commands list of the preceding step.

detector.predicate: invalid = sum(1 for i in range(1, len(steps)) if steps[i]['action'] not in steps[i-1]['admissible_commands']); total = max(1, len(steps) - 1); (invalid / total) < 0.30

unit_tests.rationale: Invalid actions are especially costly in this task because they signal the agent cannot identify the sinkbasin location or the correct clean syntax.

task_types.rationale_for_tests: Structurally identical to pick_clean_then_place_in_recep except the transformation is heating via microwave or stoveburner. The 'heat X with ...' action verb distinguishes this task. The same five-test structure (pickup, transform, place, ordering, invalid-rate) applies with heating semantics.

unit_tests.name: target_object_picked_up
unit_tests.description: Agent executed a 'take <object_target> from ...' action at least once.

detector.predicate: obj = pddl_params['object_target'].lower(); any(step['action'].lower().startswith('take ') and obj in step['action'].lower() for step in steps)

unit_tests.rationale: Picking up the target is the entry point for the heating pipeline; without it no subsequent milestone is reachable.

unit_tests.name: target_object_heated
unit_tests.description: Agent executed a 'heat <object_target> with ...' action at least once.

detector.predicate: obj = pddl_params['object_target'].lower(); any(step['action'].lower().startswith('heat ') and obj in step['action'].lower() for step in steps)

unit_tests.rationale: Heating the object is the task-distinguishing transformation; detecting it confirms the agent recognized the heat requirement.

unit_tests.name: heated_object_placed_at_receptacle
unit_tests.description: Agent executed a 'move <object_target> to <parent_target>' action at least once.

detector.predicate: obj = pddl_params['object_target'].lower(); recep = pddl_params['parent_target'].lower(); any((step['action'].lower().startswith('move ') or step['action'].lower().startswith('put ')) and obj in step['action'].lower() and recep in step['action'].lower() for step in steps)

unit_tests.rationale: Placing the heated object at the target receptacle is the terminal milestone nearest to episode completion.

unit_tests.name: correct_pipeline_order
unit_tests.description: The first take-target step occurs before the first heat-target step, and the first heat-target step occurs before the first place-target-at-recep step. All three must be detected.

detector.predicate: obj = pddl_params['object_target'].lower(); recep = pddl_params['parent_target'].lower(); pickup_i = next((i for i,s in enumerate(steps) if s['action'].lower().startswith('take ') and obj in s['action'].lower()), -1); heat_i = next((i for i,s in enumerate(steps) if s['action'].lower().startswith('heat ') and obj in s['action'].lower()), -1); place_i = next((i for i,s in enumerate(steps) if (s['action'].lower().startswith('move ') or s['action'].lower().startswith('put ')) and obj in s['action'].lower() and recep in s['action'].lower()), -1); pickup_i != -1 and heat_i != -1 and place_i != -1 and pickup_i < heat_i < place_i

unit_tests.rationale: The ordering check is the key differentiator: an agent that places before heating produces an unheated object and cannot satisfy the PDDL goal. Leakage risk is low since the correct order is already implied by the task description.

unit_tests.name: low_invalid_action_rate
unit_tests.description: Fewer than 30% of actions from step 1 onward were not in the admissible_commands list of the preceding step.

detector.predicate: invalid = sum(1 for i in range(1, len(steps)) if steps[i]['action'] not in steps[i-1]['admissible_commands']); total = max(1, len(steps) - 1); (invalid / total) < 0.30

unit_tests.rationale: Invalid actions in this task often signal the agent attempting to heat without finding the microwave or using an incorrect appliance name.

task_types.rationale_for_tests: Structurally identical to the heat variant except the transformation is cooling via fridge. The 'cool X with ...' action verb identifies the transformation. The same five-test structure applies with cooling semantics.

unit_tests.name: target_object_picked_up
unit_tests.description: Agent executed a 'take <object_target> from ...' action at least once.

detector.predicate: obj = pddl_params['object_target'].lower(); any(step['action'].lower().startswith('take ') and obj in step['action'].lower() for step in steps)

unit_tests.rationale: Picking up the target is the mandatory entry step for the cooling pipeline.

unit_tests.name: target_object_cooled
unit_tests.description: Agent executed a 'cool <object_target> with ...' action at least once.

detector.predicate: obj = pddl_params['object_target'].lower(); any(step['action'].lower().startswith('cool ') and obj in step['action'].lower() for step in steps)

unit_tests.rationale: Cooling the object is the task-distinguishing transformation; detecting it confirms the agent navigated to and used the fridge.

unit_tests.name: cooled_object_placed_at_receptacle
unit_tests.description: Agent executed a 'move <object_target> to <parent_target>' action at least once.

detector.predicate: obj = pddl_params['object_target'].lower(); recep = pddl_params['parent_target'].lower(); any((step['action'].lower().startswith('move ') or step['action'].lower().startswith('put ')) and obj in step['action'].lower() and recep in step['action'].lower() for step in steps)

unit_tests.rationale: Placing the cooled object at the target receptacle is the final milestone nearest to task completion.

unit_tests.name: correct_pipeline_order
unit_tests.description: The first take-target step occurs before the first cool-target step, and the first cool-target step occurs before the first place-target-at-recep step. All three must be detected.

detector.predicate: obj = pddl_params['object_target'].lower(); recep = pddl_params['parent_target'].lower(); pickup_i = next((i for i,s in enumerate(steps) if s['action'].lower().startswith('take ') and obj in s['action'].lower()), -1); cool_i = next((i for i,s in enumerate(steps) if s['action'].lower().startswith('cool ') and obj in s['action'].lower()), -1); place_i = next((i for i,s in enumerate(steps) if (s['action'].lower().startswith('move ') or s['action'].lower().startswith('put ')) and obj in s['action'].lower() and recep in s['action'].lower()), -1); pickup_i != -1 and cool_i != -1 and place_i != -1 and pickup_i < cool_i < place_i

unit_tests.rationale: Correct pipeline ordering is the distinguishing constraint for this task; placing before cooling violates the PDDL goal condition. Leakage risk is low since the ordering is implicit in the task description.

unit_tests.name: low_invalid_action_rate
unit_tests.description: Fewer than 30% of actions from step 1 onward were not in the admissible_commands list of the preceding step.

detector.predicate: invalid = sum(1 for i in range(1, len(steps)) if steps[i]['action'] not in steps[i-1]['admissible_commands']); total = max(1, len(steps) - 1); (invalid / total) < 0.30

unit_tests.rationale: Invalid actions in this task often indicate the agent cannot identify the fridge or uses wrong cool syntax.

task_types.rationale_for_tests: This task requires placing two instances of the target object type at the same receptacle, making it structurally different from single-object tasks. Four milestone tests track the two-object pipeline separately: first pickup, first placement, second pickup, second placement. Each is independently detectable by counting occurrences of take/place actions for the target object type. An avoided-error test on invalid actions completes the suite.

unit_tests.name: first_target_object_picked_up
unit_tests.description: At least one 'take <object_target> from ...' action appears in the trace.

detector.predicate: obj = pddl_params['object_target'].lower(); sum(1 for s in steps if s['action'].lower().startswith('take ') and obj in s['action'].lower()) >= 1

unit_tests.rationale: Picking up the first target instance is the initial milestone; an agent that never picks up the correct object type cannot progress.

unit_tests.name: first_target_placed_at_receptacle
unit_tests.description: At least one 'move <object_target> to <parent_target>' action appears in the trace.

detector.predicate: obj = pddl_params['object_target'].lower(); recep = pddl_params['parent_target'].lower(); sum(1 for s in steps if (s['action'].lower().startswith('move ') or s['action'].lower().startswith('put ')) and obj in s['action'].lower() and recep in s['action'].lower()) >= 1

unit_tests.rationale: Placing the first target object is an intermediate milestone showing the agent understands the destination receptacle.

unit_tests.name: second_target_object_picked_up
unit_tests.description: At least two 'take <object_target> from ...' actions appear in the trace (indicating the agent retrieved a second instance).

detector.predicate: obj = pddl_params['object_target'].lower(); sum(1 for s in steps if s['action'].lower().startswith('take ') and obj in s['action'].lower()) >= 2

unit_tests.rationale: Picking up a second instance of the target type is the distinguishing milestone for this task; agents that pick up one object and stop will fail here.

unit_tests.name: second_target_placed_at_receptacle
unit_tests.description: At least two 'move <object_target> to <parent_target>' actions appear in the trace (indicating both instances were placed).

detector.predicate: obj = pddl_params['object_target'].lower(); recep = pddl_params['parent_target'].lower(); sum(1 for s in steps if (s['action'].lower().startswith('move ') or s['action'].lower().startswith('put ')) and obj in s['action'].lower() and recep in s['action'].lower()) >= 2

unit_tests.rationale: Placing both target objects is the terminal milestone nearest to episode success; it receives the highest weight.

unit_tests.name: low_invalid_action_rate
unit_tests.description: Fewer than 30% of actions from step 1 onward were not in the admissible_commands list of the preceding step.

detector.predicate: invalid = sum(1 for i in range(1, len(steps)) if steps[i]['action'] not in steps[i-1]['admissible_commands']); total = max(1, len(steps) - 1); (invalid / total) < 0.30

unit_tests.rationale: Invalid actions indicate aimless exploration; this task requires efficient search for two instances so a low invalid-action rate is a meaningful quality signal.

failure_penalty.applies_when: steps[-1]['done'] == True and not any(step['won'] for step in steps)

scoring_rubric.weight_sum_policy: Per-task-type unit test weights sum to exactly 1.0; the success_bonus and failure_penalty are additive on top of this base. Maximum achievable base score is 1.0 (all tests pass), plus 0.5 success bonus = 1.5. Minimum is 0.0 (all tests fail) minus 0.1 failure penalty = -0.1.

limitations: traj_data.json loading requires game_file path in trajectory metadata; when only raw steps are passed without a game_file field, all traj_data-dependent unit tests cannot evaluate and will be marked as not-evaluated (contributing 0).

The holding field in probing trajectories is always empty string; object-holding state is inferred from admissible_commands 'move <obj>' patterns, which may misfire if the environment emits move commands for objects not actively held.

Action-based milestone detection relies on the TextWorld grammar emitting 'take', 'move', 'clean', 'heat', 'cool', 'use' as the leading verb; if a future game file changes these verb forms, the predicates will fail silently.
The invalid-action threshold of 30% is calibrated to the probing trajectories; a tighter or looser threshold may be appropriate for agents with different failure modes.
For look_at_obj_in_light, UT_LOIL_03 uses admissible_commands to infer held object; if the engine batches multiple objects in a single step's admissible list, a false positive is possible.
The failure_penalty only activates when done=True (step limit exceeded) is recorded in the trajectory; truncated recordings that stop before the environment's done signal will not incur the penalty.

PDDL facts (pddl_facts_state oracle) are not present in the probing trajectories; a richer facts-based scorer (e.g., checking isclean, ishot, iscold predicates directly) is deferred until trajectories with request_infos.facts=True are available.

For pick_two_obj_and_place, counting take-action occurrences >= 2 may overcount if the agent picks up then drops then re-picks the same instance; a stricter test would require PDDL facts to track distinct instance IDs.
